summary: Postinstall downloads and executes unverified binary from non-publisher domain
details: On `npm install`, the `postinstall` hook runs `install.js`, which fetches a platform-specific archive from a hardcoded URL on `laogou.us` (`https://laogou.us/download/veteran/v1.0.0/veteran_1.0.0_<platform>_<arch>.{tar.gz,zip}`), extracts it, chmods the resulting file 0755, and immediately executes it (`execSync("${BIN_PATH} version")`). No hash, signature, or checksum is verified against the downloaded bytes. The download host `laogou.us` does not match the package's declared publisher: `package.json` `repository.url` and `homepage` point at `github.com/veteran-cli/veteran`, while `install.js` references `yongjie0203/veteran`, and neither GitHub identity is associated with `laogou.us`. The bytes at the fetch URL are mutable, so anyone who controls `laogou.us` (now or in the future) can ship arbitrary native code to every installer's machine at install time. This is the canonical dropper shape: mutable URL + mismatched publisher + no integrity verification + immediate execution with full user privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
